text: from people selling extended warranties
but the venue is your high school gymnasium
but it's worth negative money somehow
that only discusses your relationship failures
to help them move apartments
but it's lukewarm and tastes like regret
but you have to bring your own food
but the refund is in store credit to Blockbuster
but you spend it all on shipping
but it's for a company that doesn't exist
but it's actually just a performance review
but it's from 1998 and doesn't work
about tax law exclusively
but it's actually community service
for people with worse problems than you
but it's all theoretical and useless
but the network is just you talking to yourself
but it's your ex documenting your decline
but everything is beige and disappointing
but it's about your relationship with your mother
but you still owe taxes on it
where you teach people to give up
but the entertainment is just static
for people who sell supplements
but you have to explain why you're disappointing
but all the appliances are broken
but you have to judge yourself
but the group just complains about millennials
but it's cancelled after one session
but it only covers anxiety about money
but they charge you for wasting their time
for people who have given up on love
who lectures you about your choices
but it only picks up emergency broadcasts
but you're actually the subject
that's just pictures of your failures
where you're the only patient
but it only plays during anxiety attacks
who takes more of your money
but the class is about managing disappointment
but it's actually a Ponzi scheme
that won't return your calls
but the video is about your poor choices
who seems personally offended by your existence
but the therapist is more disappointed than you are
but they only take appointments during your work hours
but the game involves explaining your life failures
for the sequel to Cats
while he mansplains blockchain technology
about tax preparation methods
where you learn you're the disappointment
about people more successful than you
that reveals your browser history
but everyone just wants to sell you insurance
at a 2% annual rate
but it gets cancelled for being too depressing
but they only call during dinner
that costs more than your monthly rent
assuming you can handle mild criticism
whose powers include crippling student debt
for the worst video ever made
while your relatives ask when you're getting married
but it's all in quarters from 1987
where you play the villain's accountant
but it's just him reading his grocery list
for people who collect participation trophies
but you're the backup dancer for rejection
by falling into an open manhole
while they're busy promoting their new tax preparation service
with your high school guidance counselor as co-host
for making people feel better about themselves
whose role is getting killed by the opening credits
about proper investment strategies
for "Most Disappointing Sequel to Your Life"
assuming anyone watches television anymore
whose superpower is explaining why everyone's disappointed
but it's just elevator music being played badly
while your family explains where you went wrong
that's somehow pay-to-win
of disappointment and student loans
but it's just Gordon Ramsay explaining your life choices
about accepting mediocrity
with people who vote for reality TV stars
whose only qualification is being related to Batman
assuming they don't fact-check you live on air
to discuss your relationship with your parents
for helping people feel superior
but he makes a three-hour documentary about your poor choices
assuming you don't have a panic attack from mild spice
but they're on strike indefinitely
while he just points out everything wrong with your appearance
whose special talent is disappointing everyone immediately
while he explains all the ways you've failed as a person
based on their ability to handle disappointment
that are just you wandering around Walmart
but he just roasts your personality for two hours
as the person who can't figure out how elevators work
for "Most Creative Use of Disappointment"
assuming you don't cry during the talent portion
but it's decaf and room temperature
